title: Sub-Meter Audits: Checking Your Water and Electricity Connections Before the Unpacking Team Leaves
excerpt: Sub-meter audits new apartment: check water electricity connections before unpacking team leaves, utility verification move-in Addis Ababa.
content:
Sub-meter audits: checking your water and electricity connections before the unpacking team leaves

Sub-metered units bill your actual consumption—if water trickles or breaker trips mid-unload, dispute later bleeds into deposit fights and vendor blame games. Audit meters and connections before unpacking team leaves while truck still onsite and witnesses available.

This guide audits sub-meters and utility connections at move-in before full unpack.

Electricity audit steps

  • Photograph meter reading timestamp day one.
  • Test every room socket with phone charger or tester.
  • Run stove oven if gas-electric hybrid verify.
  • AC unit startup summer critical.
  • Label breaker map if panel unclear.
  • Report dead circuit management before furniture blocks access.

Water audit steps

Hot and cold all taps; flush toilet fill cycle; shower pressure; kitchen sink flow; sub-meter spin verify not stuck; leak under sink visual.

Documentation for billing

Compare move-in reading management log; email photo same day; retain utility account transfer receipt; dispute window know lease clause.

Unpacking team coordination

Pause heavy appliance plug until audit done; washer trial load if time; don't mount TV until outlet confirmed dedicated circuit sometimes required.

Active Movers move-in pause

Crew briefed ten-minute utility hold standard new build; escalate management contact if fault found.

Conclusion

Sub-meter audit before unpack closes billing disputes early—water and electricity verified while movers witness not month-two surprise bill.
author: Active Movers Team
